title: AI Product Engineer (People Experience)
company_focus:
Britain and Ireland's largest commercial news publisher with
120+ media brands
description:
Company Description

About us

Hello, we’re Reach. Every day, our trusted news, stories and
content connect with millions of people where they live -
whether that’s on their social feed or at their
neighbourhood newsstand; through their local community or
their take on the hot topic of the day.

We're home to more than 120 brands - from household names
like the Mirror, Express, DailyStar and Daily Record, to
local titles like MyLondon, BelfastLive, and the Manchester
Evening News, and brands making a splash in the US like the
Irish Star.

It's our ability to understand people and the things that
matter to them that makes us Britain and Ireland's largest
commercial news publisher. We reach around 70% of the UK's
online population, 10% of the US online population, and over
100 million social followers around the world, connecting
with them on who they are, where they live and what they
love.

Your story, with Reach

As a modern media organisation, we're much more than just
news. And it takes a wide range of people and skills to
serve diverse audiences, customers and communities like we
do. We know everyone has their own story to tell, so we’ve
built an environment where every background is respected and
every day is a day to explore. We’ll help you build a career
that works with your life, while giving you the space to
create and grow.

About this role

Are you ready to build the future of People Experience?

We are looking for an AI Product Engineer (our internal "AI
Builder") to join our People Tech, Data & Innovation team.
This isn’t a traditional HR role. We want a tech-forward
creator who believes that AI and automation are the keys to
unlocking human potential.

You will be the bridge between complex data and seamless
employee experiences, using the Google ecosystem to turn
manual "clutter" into high-tech, automated solutions.

What You’ll Be Doing

- AI Solution Architecting: You’ll use Gemini and other LLMs
  to generate Google Apps Script/JavaScript code, connecting
  our systems and building bespoke internal tools that make
  "self-service" a reality.
- Google Cloud Innovation: You will leverage Google Cloud
  Project environments to manage data and build structured
  workflows that capture clean data at the source.
- Data Storytelling: You’ll deep-dive into PX insights to
  craft stunning Looker Studio or Tableau dashboards,
  translating technical data into "plain English" narratives
  for our Chief People Officer and senior stakeholders.
- Automation Mastery: You’ll proactively hunt down manual
  processes and "email tennis," replacing them with
  automated workflows (via Zapier, Power Automate, or
  scripts) to boost productivity across the business.
- Strategic Foundation: You’ll act as the primary data
  supplier to our Workforce Planning function, ensuring our
  "Operational Data" is clean and ready for future
  predictive modeling.

What You’ll Need to Succeed

- Google Ecosystem Expertise: You are comfortable within
  Google Cloud Project and have a strong aptitude for Google
  Apps Script.
- AI-Fluent: You are an expert at prompting LLMs (like Gemini)
  to write code, debug errors, and structure logic. You
  don’t just use AI; you partner with it.
- Data Visualization Pro: You have proven mastery of Looker
  Studio or Tableau, with the ability to turn messy datasets
  (BigQuery/Sheets) into intuitive stories.
- The "Better Way" Mindset: You are naturally proactive. You
  don't need all the answers on day one, but you have the
  technical curiosity to find them.
- Low-Code Aptitude: You don’t need to be a full-stack
  developer, but you must be comfortable reading, editing,
  and implementing code to solve problems.

How we'll look after you

We take good care of our people - and that means more than
just a pay packet. Whatever your role, you'll get a range of
benefits, including:

- 25 days' holiday (plus bank holidays), as well as an extra
  day after you've been with us for three, five and 10
  years. We also have a scheme to help you buy extra holiday
  if you want to.
- An extra day's paid leave each year to volunteer for a cause
  close to your community or your heart.
- Big moment day - an extra day’s paid leave for a moment that
  matters to you - like getting married, moving house or
  welcoming a new addition to the family (human or furry!)
- Bonus scheme - you'll get a stake in our success and a
  chance to add to your annual earnings.
- Help saving for your retirement - we'll match your company
  pension contributions up to 6% and you can contribute
  through salary sacrifice.
- Supportive and progressive people policies - we’re proud to
  have a range of policies to support people in their lives
  in and outside work, including enhanced family leave and
  policies to promote flexibility, inclusion and
  progression. We believe you shouldn’t have to wait to join
  us to know what to expect from us, which is why we share
  this overview of the policies we have in place to support
  families and carers. If there's something you want to
  understand more about, just ask - it won’t make a
  difference to your application.
- A healthcare cash plan - giving you money back for a range
  of medical expenses and fast access to doctor's
  appointments.
- Car lease scheme - you can buy a brand new electric or
  hybrid car through our leasing partner, and pay for it
  through salary sacrifice.
- Wellbeing support - including a 24/7 assistance programme
  for you and your family.
- Access to lots of discounts - including 15% off with Reach's
  very own online marketplace, Yimbly, where you can buy
  everything from homewares to beauty products.
- Interest-free loans - including for season tickets and
  technology

Additional Information

If you’re excited about this role but don’t tick every
single box, please don’t let that stop you from applying.

We know that everyone has their own story to tell, and we
value potential, passion and the perspectives that different
backgrounds bring to our work.

Diverse perspectives strengthen our teams and help us better
serve our audiences, which is why we’re dedicated to
fostering an inclusive workplace where everyone feels
welcome and supported. As an equal opportunities employer,
we encourage applications from people of all backgrounds and
experiences.

We are committed to a recruitment process that is fair and
welcoming to all, without discrimination on the basis of
age, disability, gender identity, ethnicity, religion or
belief, sexual orientation, or any other protected
characteristic.
responsibilities: Use Gemini and other LLMs to generate Google Apps Script/JavaScript code for building bespoke internal tools and self-service solutions
Leverage Google Cloud Project environments to manage data and build structured workflows that capture clean data
Create data visualizations and dashboards using Looker Studio or Tableau to translate technical data into narratives for stakeholders
Identify and automate manual processes using Zapier, Power Automate, or custom scripts
Act as primary data supplier to Workforce Planning function, ensuring operational data is clean and ready for predictive modeling
use_cases: Internal HR and People Experience automation
Workforce planning and predictive modeling data preparation
Manual process automation to boost productivity
Data visualization for executive stakeholder reporting
ai_classification_reasoning:
This role actively uses LLMs (Gemini) to generate code,
build internal tools, and architect AI solutions for
automation. The engineer directly deploys AI/LLM
capabilities to create working systems, not just supporting
infrastructure for others' AI work.
